subject: Accounting
audience: Students learning the logic of financial statements

units.name: Transaction Lens
units.metaphor: Seeing the two-sided effect of every business event
units.description: Students learn to translate real events into accounting movement.

units.name: Ledger Engine
units.metaphor: Keeping the machine balanced as values move
units.description: Students practice debits, credits, and account types.

units.name: Statement Bridge
units.metaphor: Connecting entries to the story of the business
units.description: Students see how transactions become statements.

concepts.name: Accounting Equation
concepts.story: The accounting equation is the balance beam under the whole subject.
concepts.grit_prompt: When a transaction feels confusing, return to the beam. Something moved, but the system still balances.
concepts.visual_metaphor: A balance scale holding assets on one side and claims on the other.

ingredients.label: Claims on assets
ingredients.description: Liabilities and equity explain who has a claim on company resources.
ingredients.failure_mode: The student treats equity as cash instead of ownership claim.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Classify who has a claim after a simple purchase or loan.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: Colored claim tags attached to a stack of resources.

concepts.name: Debits and Credits
concepts.description: Debit and credit are directions in the ledger, not good and bad.
concepts.story: A ledger is a map. Debit and credit are left and right turns.
concepts.grit_prompt: Mixing up debit and credit is normal at first. Each correction strengthens the direction sense.
concepts.visual_metaphor: Two lanes carrying values through a ledger machine.

ingredients.label: Normal balance
ingredients.description: Each account type has a side where increases usually live.
ingredients.failure_mode: The student memorizes isolated rules without account type logic.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Predict the normal balance for asset, liability, equity, revenue, and expense accounts.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: Account types docking on their home side of the ledger.

concepts.name: Journal Entries
concepts.description: Recording transactions with accounts, debit amounts, and credit amounts.
concepts.story: A journal entry is a tiny story of what changed and why the books still balance.
concepts.grit_prompt: A wrong entry is useful data. Find the account, find the side, rebalance.
concepts.visual_metaphor: A transaction flowing into two equal columns.

ingredients.label: Dual effect
ingredients.description: Every transaction affects at least two accounts.
ingredients.failure_mode: The student records only one side of a transaction.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Name both accounts affected by a cash purchase, sale, or loan.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: One spark lighting two connected nodes.

concepts.name: Financial Statement Flow
concepts.description: Understanding how account balances feed into financial statements.
concepts.story: Statements are the final story the ledger tells about the business.
concepts.grit_prompt: If the statements do not make sense yet, trace one account at a time. Clarity compounds.
concepts.visual_metaphor: Ledger streams merging into three clear reports.

ingredients.label: Statement destination
ingredients.description: Each account type belongs on a particular statement.
ingredients.failure_mode: The student puts revenue, assets, and expenses on the wrong report.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Route accounts to income statement, balance sheet, or retained earnings.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: Accounts moving down labeled channels into reports.
